title: How accurate are population estimates and projections? | Stats NZ
content:
## How accurate are population estimates and projections?
26 September 2016, 10:45am
22 June 2023: We have updated table 8.6 to correct the most under-projected and over-projected ALBA and associated 'RE %' figures – 10 years out from base population – for the projection '1996-base completed 2000'.
We have corrected the interactive Excel file under 'Download documents' for incorrect referencing of ‘low’ projections for the period 1996–2006 in the table of Error and Relative Error of medium projections in the worksheet tab for 'Projections', and for another minor formula referencing issue in this table. We also updated table footnotes in both the 'Estimates' and 'Projections' tabs.
How accurate are population estimates and projections? An evaluation of Statistics New Zealand population estimates and projections, 1996–2013 evaluates the accuracy of recent national and subnational population estimates and projections.
The report focuses on estimates and projections of the total population produced and published since 1996, although earlier projections are included where practicable.
It is designed to help customers understand the accuracy of Stats NZ’s population estimates and projections relative to observed populations, the reasons for inaccuracies, and discusses current developments that may improve accuracy.
- Accuracy of estimated and projected populations 1996–2013 – tables Excel spreadsheet, 162 KB
